trade.summary: Substantial bilateral trade under a Canada-Corea del Sud FTA (since 2015). Canada exports LNG, coal, uranium, canola, and copper to Corea del Sud. Corea del Sud exports vehicles (Hyundai, Kia), electronics (Samsung, LG), ships, and machinery to Canada.

diplomatic.summary: Strong bilateral relations deepened by the Corea del Sud-Canada FTA and shared security interests in the Indo-Pacific. Both are committed to the Stati Uniti-led alliance system and share concerns about North Corea del Sud and Cina. Active diplomatic calendar and growing strategic dialogue.

military.summary: Growing defense cooperation. Canada participates in Corea del Sudn peninsula security discussions. Canadian troops participated in Corea del Sudn War (significant historical bond). Joint exercises in Indo-Pacific. Growing defense industrial cooperation.

financial.summary: Growing bilateral investment. Corea del Sudn EV battery makers (Samsung SDI, LG Energy Solution, SK On) are investing massively in Canadian battery manufacturing to access Stati UnitiMCA markets. Canadian pension funds invest in Corea del Sudn assets.

energy.summary: Corea del Sud is a major buyer of Canadian LNG and coal. Corea del Sudn companies are investing in Canadian LNG infrastructure. Canada sees Corea del Sud as a key energy partner in Asia. The energy relationship is strategically important for both countries.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation. Collaboration in nuclear energy (CANDU expertise, SMRs), battery technology, and semiconductor research. Canadian and Corea del Sudn universities have exchange programs.
